places.order: Women's classic clothing at outlet prices: dresses, knitwear, blouses, trousers; brands like Bon'A Parte, Kaffe, Saint Tropez, B.Young, Cream, ICHI
places.famousFor: Bon'A Parte's own outlet store on Langelinie, selling discounted women's fashion alongside Scandinavian sister brands
places.goodToKnow: A single-brand-group outlet shop, not a mall; womenswear focused · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Copenhagen Designer Outlet
places.near: Høje-Taastrup, ~20 min drive from Copenhagen; right by Høje Taastrup station
places.order: Branded fashion at 30-70% off: Gant, Hugo Boss, Calvin Klein, Tommy Hilfiger, plus Danish design (Royal Copenhagen, Imerco)
places.famousFor: Denmark's largest outlet, integrated with the City2 mall in Greater Copenhagen; 80+ stores
places.goodToKnow: Indoor outlet inside/next to City2; easily reached by train; family-friendly · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Ringsted Outlet
places.near: Ringsted (Zealand); ~35-45 min from Copenhagen, direct trains to Ringsted station then short transfer
places.order: Nordic and international fashion and sportswear (BOSS, Nike, GANT, Peak Performance, Le Creuset) at 30-70% off
places.famousFor: Denmark's only true outlet village; ~38 international brand outlets, 45 min from Copenhagen
places.goodToKnow: Marketed as Ringsted Designer Outlet; the only outlet village in Denmark · Location needs verification · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Royal Copenhagen - Outlet
places.near: Sondre Fasanvej 9, Frederiksberg; about 4 km from Copenhagen City Hall Square
places.order: Royal Copenhagen porcelain (esp. Blue Fluted), seconds/'2. Sort' pieces, plus Georg Jensen upstairs
places.famousFor: Royal Copenhagen porcelain factory outlet in Frederiksberg; seconds and discontinued 'white gold' pieces
places.goodToKnow: Best value is on second-sorting items with tiny blemishes (red '2. Sort' label); Georg Jensen outlet on the upper level · Kids: Skip for kids (porcelain)

places.name: IC Factory Outlet
places.near: Raffinaderivej 10, 2300 København S (Amager Øst)
places.order: Discounted Scandinavian clothing and accessories from IC Group labels at affordable prices
places.famousFor: Factory outlet for Danish fashion house IC Group (brands such as InWear and Tiger of Sweden) in Copenhagen's Amager district
places.goodToKnow: Open Mon-Fri 10:00-18:00, Sat 10:00-17:00, Sun 11:00-16:00; wheelchair-accessible entrance · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Magasin du Nord
places.near: Kongens Nytorv 13, central Copenhagen; M3/M4 Metro and Strøget nearby
places.order: Designer and Scandinavian fashion, beauty, homeware and Danish design; well-known food hall in the basement
places.famousFor: Denmark's most iconic upscale department store, the grand Magasin du Nord flagship on Kongens Nytorv (founded 1868)
places.goodToKnow: Historic landmark building; a flagship of the Magasin du Nord chain. Tax-free shopping for non-EU visitors · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Reffen - Copenhagen Street Food
places.near: Refshalevej 167, Refshaleøen (former industrial harbour area); harbour bus 991/992 or bus 2A, ~10 min from the centre
places.order: Global street food from dozens of stalls, craft beer and cocktails; some design/craft maker stalls too
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's largest street-food market, a sustainability-minded outdoor market of container kitchens and makers on Refshaleøen
places.goodToKnow: Seasonal — mainly open spring to autumn; outdoor, harbourside, lively in good weather. Eat-and-browse rather than retail shopping · Kids: All ages

places.name: The LEGO® Store Tivoli Gardens
places.near: Inside Tivoli Gardens, Vesterbrogade 3, beside a Tivoli entrance; opposite Copenhagen Central Station
places.order: LEGO sets, exclusive Tivoli/Copenhagen items, Pick-A-Brick, and a custom minifigure (torso, legs, head, hat, accessories)
places.famousFor: LEGO Store inside Tivoli Gardens, home to the rare LEGO Minifigure Factory where you build a custom minifigure
places.goodToKnow: Minifigure Factory is a highlight found in only a few stores worldwide; note entry/hours follow Tivoli's calendar · Kids: All ages

places.name: Lego Store Copenhagen
places.near: Vimmelskaftet 37 on the Strøget pedestrian street, central Copenhagen; near Nørreport/Kongens Nytorv
places.order: LEGO sets (including large/exclusive ones), Pick-A-Brick loose bricks by the cup, and Copenhagen-themed items
places.famousFor: The LEGO flagship store on Strøget — a three-zone retail space with a Pick-A-Brick wall and a central 'Living Room' build area
places.goodToKnow: The main city-centre flagship (distinct from the Tivoli store); great for families, gets busy on weekends · Kids: All ages

places.name: Studio Arhoj
places.near: Skindergade 7, downtown Copenhagen (near Strøget/Nørreport)
places.order: The signature Ghost and Familia figurines, glass Blobs, and hand-glazed mugs/cups (Chug Mug, Sip Cups) and tableware
places.famousFor: Anders Arhoj's Copenhagen ceramics studio/shop, famous worldwide for colourful drip-glazed 'Ghost' figurines blending Danish and Japanese design
places.goodToKnow: Each piece is handmade and unique so stock varies; small store/studio — a great spot for one-of-a-kind Danish design gifts · Kids: Skip for kids (fragile shop)

places.name: TorvehallerneKBH
places.near: Frederiksborggade 21, Israels Plads; right by Nørreport Station (Metro/S-train)
places.order: Smørrebrød, fresh fish and cheese, coffee, chocolate, and the famous Hallernes smørrebrød and Coffee Collective beans
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's beloved glass-and-steel food market (two halls) at Israels Plads, a foodie destination of ~60 stalls
places.goodToKnow: Open daily (shorter Sunday hours); mix of eat-here stalls and gourmet groceries. Great for a graze-and-shop lunch · Kids: All ages

places.name: Kongens Nytorv
places.near: Adjacent to Nyhavn; Kongens Nytorv metro station
places.order: Photograph the equestrian statue and Nyhavn at one corner; café terraces and luxury hotel
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's largest and grandest public square, ringed by the Royal Theatre and Hotel d'Angleterre
places.goodToKnow: Becomes an ice rink in winter. Central hub linking Nyhavn and Strøget. · Kids: All ages

places.name: Købmagergade
places.near: Off Strøget, central Copenhagen; near Nørreport station
places.order: High-street and Danish design shops; climb the Round Tower for city views
places.famousFor: Major pedestrian shopping street branching off Strøget, home to the Round Tower (Rundetårn)
places.goodToKnow: Car-free and busy; part of the world's longest pedestrian shopping zone. · Kids: All ages

places.name: Strøget
places.order: Shop high-street and Danish design stores, watch street performers, branch off to Illum and the side streets
places.famousFor: Strøget — one of Europe's longest pedestrian shopping streets, running through central Copenhagen from City Hall Square to Kongens Nytorv
places.goodToKnow: Car-free; mixes budget chains at one end with luxury near Kongens Nytorv · Kids: All ages

places.name: Fisketorvet — Copenhagen Mall
places.near: Kalvebod Brygge on the harbour ring road — huge in-house car park, easiest mall to drive to in the city
places.famousFor: Big waterfront mall with ~120 stores (H&M, Zara, sports, toys), food court and CinemaxX — the practical rainy-day / family-restock stop
places.goodToKnow: Shops typically daily ~10:00-20:00; harbour swimming spot Kalvebod Bølge boardwalk just outside · Kids: All ages (mall)

places.name: Illum
places.near: On Strøget at Amagertorv; park at Q-Park Industriens Hus and walk
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's luxury department store (1891) on Amagertorv — Danish design floors and a rooftop food hall with free views over Strøget's spires
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~10-19 (Sat/Sun vary); rooftop terrace = free photo spot facing the Stork Fountain (saved); tax-free shopping desk for non-EU visitors · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Field's Copenhagen
places.near: Ørestad, right off the E20 motorway to the Øresund Bridge — thousands of free parking spaces; natural stop before/after Malmö or Den Blå Planet
places.famousFor: One of Scandinavia's largest malls — 140+ stores over 3 floors with big kids' play zones and a large food level
places.goodToKnow: Open daily ~10-20; more space and easier with a 9-seat van than city-centre shopping · Kids: All ages (mall)

places.name: Jægersborggade
places.near: Nørrebro, beside Assistens Cemetery; combine with Superkilen 5 min away
places.famousFor: Hidden-gem shopping street: ceramics studios, Karamelleriet caramel-boilers you can watch, vintage shops and cult bakeries along one photogenic block
places.goodToKnow: Shops ~11-18; the family's saved café The Sixteen Twelve is on this street — good coffee anchor while browsing · Kids: Adults & teens

places.name: Emporia Shopping Centre
places.near: Hyllie, literally the FIRST exit after the Øresund Bridge — easiest Sweden stop with the van; huge parking garage
places.famousFor: 200-store mega-mall behind a curving amber-glass entrance (an architecture photo in itself) with a free 27,000 m² rooftop sky park
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 10-20; prices in SEK (often favourable vs DKK); rooftop park is free and has bridge views — good leg-stretch for the kids · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (mall)

places.name: Amalienborg Palace
places.near: Frederiksstaden, central Copenhagen; walkable, near the harbour
places.order: The royal guard in front of a palace facade; the octagonal square with the equestrian statue centred and the Marble Church dome behind
places.famousFor: Amalienborg, the Danish royal residence: four identical rococo palaces around an octagonal square with a guard-changing ceremony
places.goodToKnow: Guard change daily around noon; free to enter the square; aligns with the Marble Church and Opera House · Kids: All ages (guard change)

places.name: Kastellet
places.near: Near Churchillparken and the Little Mermaid, central Copenhagen; walkable
places.order: Aerial-style or elevated shot of the five-pointed star ramparts; the historic windmill on the green bastion
places.famousFor: Kastellet, a remarkably preserved star-shaped 17th-century citadel with grassy ramparts, a windmill and rows of red barracks
places.goodToKnow: Free public park; lovely in morning light; star shape is best appreciated from a high/aerial angle · Kids: All ages; stroller-friendly

places.name: Church of Our Saviour
places.near: Christianshavn, Copenhagen; walkable, near Christiania
places.order: The corkscrew golden spire from below; panoramic city-and-harbour view from the top of the external stairs
places.famousFor: Church of Our Saviour (Vor Frelsers Kirke), famous for its black-and-gold helix spire with an external staircase you can climb
places.goodToKnow: Tower climb has a fee and seasonal hours (400 steps, last 150 outdoors); clear days give the best summit view · Kids: Spire 9+; 5yo skip climb

places.name: Memorial Anchor
places.near: At the head of Nyhavn by the harbour, central Copenhagen; walkable
places.order: The big black anchor with the colourful Nyhavn houses or harbour behind it
places.famousFor: Mindeankeret, a large memorial anchor by Nyhavn honouring Danish sailors who died in WWII
places.goodToKnow: Free, always accessible; pairs with the classic Nyhavn waterfront shot just steps away · Kids: All ages (photo stop)

places.name: Frederik's Church
places.near: Frederiksstaden, central Copenhagen, just west of Amalienborg; walkable
places.order: The massive green dome framed straight down the Amalienborg axis; the columned facade; ornate interior dome from below
places.famousFor: Frederik's Church, known as the Marble Church, with the largest church dome in Scandinavia, sitting on the axis of Amalienborg
places.goodToKnow: Free to enter the church; dome climb at set times for a city view; lines up perfectly with the palace square · Kids: All ages (quick visit)

places.name: Nyboder
places.near: Near Kastellet, central Copenhagen; walkable
places.order: A long perspective down a street of identical yellow houses; a single doorway/detail of the ochre facades
places.famousFor: Nyboder, a historic district of long ochre-yellow terraced row houses built for the navy, with picturesque uniform streets
places.goodToKnow: Residential, so be discreet; the yellow walls glow in soft sun; quiet and uncrowded · Kids: All ages (stroll)

places.name: Det Kgl. Bibliotek, The Royal Library
places.near: Slotsholmen waterfront, central Copenhagen; walkable
places.order: The angular black facade reflecting the harbour; the dramatic atrium with its glass wall and walkways inside
places.famousFor: The Royal Library's 'Black Diamond' extension, a sleek black-granite-and-glass building leaning over the waterfront
places.goodToKnow: Free to enter the atrium; reflective facade shines at golden hour; interior open during library hours · Kids: Best 9+; quiet inside

places.name: Christiansborg Palace
places.near: Slotsholmen islet, central Copenhagen; walkable
places.order: The grand palace facade and courtyard; 360-degree rooftop city panorama from the free tower
places.famousFor: Christiansborg Palace, seat of the Danish Parliament, whose tower is the tallest in Copenhagen and free to ascend
places.goodToKnow: Tower is free but closed Mondays; best skyline light late afternoon; can be busy · Kids: Best 9+; tower lift all ages

places.name: The Tube
places.near: By Nordhavn metro/S-train station, Copenhagen; reachable by metro
places.order: Long symmetrical shot down the tunnel showing the colour gradient and moving walkways; a figure centred for scale
places.famousFor: Colourful pedestrian Metro tunnel at Nordhavn/Marmormolen, lined in 14 shifting shades from burgundy to grey, a striking modern photo spot
places.goodToKnow: Free and indoors so weather-proof; quieter off-peak; colours run redder toward the S-train, greyer toward the Metro · Kids: All ages (quick art stop)

places.name: City Hall Square
places.near: Central Copenhagen at the start of Stroget and near Tivoli; walkable
places.order: The City Hall facade and tower across the open square; the plaza fountains and statues
places.famousFor: City Hall Square (Radhuspladsen), a large central plaza fronted by the red-brick Copenhagen City Hall and its clock tower
places.goodToKnow: Free, lively public square; City Hall tower can be climbed on tours; busy hub day and night · Kids: All ages

places.name: The Round Tower
places.near: Central Copenhagen off Kobmagergade; walkable
places.order: The curving cobbled spiral ramp inside; rooftop panorama over Copenhagen's rooftops and spires
places.famousFor: The Round Tower (Rundetaarn), a 17th-century tower with a unique spiral ramp and a rooftop platform offering old-town views
places.goodToKnow: Small entry fee; the helical ramp and the rooftop are the two key shots; clear days best for the view · Kids: All ages; ramp, no stairs

places.name: Copenhagen Contemporary
places.near: Refshalevej, Refshaleøen; harbour bus 992 or bus 2A, or cycle across from the center; ticketed
places.order: Rotating large-scale immersive installations; the raw industrial hall interiors; harbour-front views of the dock district
places.famousFor: Large contemporary art center for immersive installation art in a converted industrial welding hall on Refshaleøen
places.goodToKnow: Entry ticketed; exhibits change so check what's on; the raw warehouse setting itself is very photogenic · Kids: Best 9+

places.name: Stork Fountain
places.near: Amagertorv, on the Strøget pedestrian street in the old town; central, fully walkable
places.order: The bronze storks atop the tiered fountain; the fountain framed by Amagertorv's historic gabled facades
places.famousFor: Ornate 1894 Renaissance-style fountain with bronze storks on Amagertorv along the Strøget shopping street
places.goodToKnow: Free, outdoor, always open; a busy meeting point so wait for a gap in foot traffic; nice in afternoon light · Kids: All ages (photo stop)

places.name: Grundtvig's Church
places.near: ~15 min drive north-west of centre; free street parking around På Bjerget
places.famousFor: Cathedral-scale expressionist church built from 6 million yellow bricks — the symmetrical nave shot is one of Copenhagen's most famous photos
places.goodToKnow: Free entry; typically open Mon-Sat daytime, limited Sunday hours around services — check site; interior photography allowed · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (quick visit)

places.name: Turning Torso & Västra Hamnen boardwalk
places.near: ~45 min drive from Copenhagen over the Øresund Bridge; parking garages in Västra Hamnen
places.famousFor: Calatrava's 190 m twisting tower — Scandinavia's tallest building — above the Scaniabadet boardwalk where locals dive into the Öresund; sunset photo heaven
places.goodToKnow: Tower is residential (no public entry) — the photo is from the boardwalk; bring swimwear, the wooden decks are a free bathing spot; bridge toll applies each way (vans 6 m+ pay a higher class — check oresundsbron.com; carry passports) · Location needs verification · Kids: All ages (boardwalk)

places.name: Hamad Bin Khalifa Civilisation Centre (Grand Mosque of Copenhagen)
places.near: Metro M3 from Nørreport to Skjolds Plads, then ~8 min walk; taxi ~10 min
places.notes: Wikipedia (Hamad Bin Khalifa Civilisation Center) + Wikidata Q16957737, checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Denmark's largest mosque and its first with a minaret (2014, Qatari-funded) — 3,000 worshippers indoors plus 1,500 in the courtyard; titanium-and-glass exterior; the natural Friday-prayer choice for a visiting family
places.goodToKnow: Sunni; run by the Danish Islamic Council. No outdoor adhan (agreed with the city) — small courtyard speakers only. Women's section and wudu facilities; arrive early for jum'ah in summer

places.name: Imam Ali Mosque
places.near: S-train or Metro M3 to Nørrebro Station, then ~10 min walk via Frederikssundsvej
places.notes: Wikipedia (Imam Ali Mosque Copenhagen) + Yelp listing Vibevej, checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Denmark's only purpose-built Shia mosque and one of its most beautiful — neo-Iranian style with a turquoise dome and twin 32 m minarets; capacity 1,500; opened 2015
places.goodToKnow: Shia congregation but visitors of all backgrounds welcome; worth seeing architecturally even if you pray jum'ah elsewhere

places.name: Masjid Al-Faruq
places.near: Bus 5C along Nørrebrogade or S-train/M3 to Nørrebro Station, then short walk
places.famousFor: Busy Sunni neighbourhood masjid in the heart of Muslim Nørrebro (2014) — convenient for daily prayers when you're shopping on Nørrebrogade
places.goodToKnow: Wudu facilities and restrooms; crowded at jum'ah. A plain community mosque — go for prayer, not sightseeing

places.name: Dansk Islamisk Center
places.near: Metro M1/M2 toward Vanløse to Lindevang, then short walk
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's Danish-language mosque community — khutbahs and activities in Danish/English, known as especially welcoming to visitors and families
places.goodToKnow: Prayers in Arabic as usual, everything else in Danish; check dicenter.dk for jum'ah times before going · Location needs verification

places.name: Netto Fiolstræde
places.near: 2 min walk from Nørreport Station exits, in the Latin Quarter
places.famousFor: The closest proper discount supermarket to Nørreport Station — water, fruit, snacks, nappies and basics at the lowest prices in the old town
places.goodToKnow: Open daily approx. 07:00-22:00. Note: the Irma chain was wound down by Coop in 2023, so Netto/Føtex are the practical options now. Card/Apple Pay everywhere

places.name: Føtex City Nørrebrogade
places.near: ~10 min walk from Nørreport across the lakes bridge, or bus 5C one stop
places.notes: foetex.dk store finder (Føtex City Nørrebrogade), checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Full-range supermarket nearest the Nørreport area — bigger selection than Netto (bakery, fresh fruit, baby items, household), good for stocking a family apartment
places.goodToKnow: Open daily 07:00-22:00; PostNord parcel counter inside. Halal-labelled chicken/lamb sometimes stocked, but the halal butchers further up Nørrebrogade are more reliable

places.name: Torvehallerne Food Market
places.near: Directly beside Nørreport Station, Israels Plads side
places.notes: Well-known landmark at Israels Plads/Nørreport; hours per torvehallernekbh.dk, verify on arrival
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's famous glass food halls right at Nørreport — fresh fruit, fish, nuts, dates, spices, juice and quality picnic supplies; doubles as a fun family stop
places.goodToKnow: Pricier than supermarkets; most stalls close around 19:00-20:00 (earlier Sundays). Great for fresh seafood and fruit for the apartment

places.name: Alhoda Halal Slagter + the Nørrebrogade halal row
places.near: Bus 5C from Nørreport along Nørrebrogade (~10 min), or M3/S-train to Nørrebro Station and walk back down the street
places.famousFor: Copenhagen's first halal butcher (since 1988) — Danish beef, lamb and fresh poultry; the surrounding stretch of outer Nørrebrogade is lined with Middle Eastern/Turkish grocers, bakeries and sweet shops
places.goodToKnow: Cash sometimes preferred at the small grocers; best produce prices in the city. Combine with jum'ah at Al-Faruq or the Grand Mosque nearby · Location needs verification
places.address: Nørrebrogade 193 (second branch at Nørrebrogade 223), 2200 København N

places.name: 7-Eleven Nørreport Station (late-night + SIM cards)
places.near: Inside/at Nørreport Station
places.notes: Traveltomtom + yourdenmarkguide SIM guides (7-Eleven sells Lebara/Lycamobile), checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Round-the-clock convenience store at your home station — milk, bread, snacks, and the main place tourists buy Danish prepaid SIMs (Lebara and Lycamobile sold and topped up here)
places.goodToKnow: Lycamobile tourist SIM ~29 DKK with data bundle, Lebara ~49 DKK; passport not required for prepaid. A Netto near Rådhuspladsen (Vestergade) is reported open 24h if you need a real supermarket at night — verify locally · Location needs verification
places.address: Nørreport Station, Nørre Voldgade, 1358 København K

places.name: Steno Apotek (24-hour pharmacy)
places.near: Metro M3 from Nørreport to København H (2 stops) or S-train; exit toward Vesterbrogade
places.famousFor: Denmark's only 24/7 pharmacy — open 24 hours a day, 365 days a year, directly across from Copenhagen Central Station; the address for any middle-of-the-night fever or medicine need
places.goodToKnow: Phone +45 33 14 82 66. Small night surcharge applies after normal hours; EU/foreign prescriptions handled — for Danish prescriptions call 1813 first and they send it electronically

places.name: Nørrebro Apotek
places.near: Walk from Nørreport across Dronning Louises Bro (~10 min); bus 5C stops outside
places.famousFor: Handy everyday pharmacy 10 minutes' walk from Nørreport, open daily including Sundays — paracetamol, sunscreen, kids' medicine without the trip to Central Station
places.goodToKnow: Open approx. 08:00-20:00 Mon-Sun (phone +45 35 39 83 82). Many common medicines (ibuprofen etc.) are pharmacy-only in Denmark, not supermarket items

places.name: Medical Helpline 1813 (call BEFORE any ER visit)
places.near: Dial +45 1813 from any phone (use the +45 prefix on a UAE SIM); life-threatening emergencies dial 112
places.famousFor: The Capital Region's out-of-hours medical line — nurses/doctors who speak English triage you by phone and book you into the right emergency department or urgent-care clinic; tourists are fully entitled to use it
places.goodToKnow: Danish ERs generally will not admit walk-ins without a 1813 referral — always call first and they give you a time slot, which massively cuts waiting with children. Acute care for visitors is free · Location needs verification

places.name: Bispebjerg Hospital Akutmodtagelse (nearest adult ER)
places.near: Taxi ~10-15 min from Nørreport, or S-train to Bispebjerg Station; go only after calling 1813
places.notes: Apple Maps/vymaps Bispebjerg akutmodtagelse listing + visitcopenhagen emergencies page, checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: The 24/7 emergency department serving central Copenhagen adults — where 1813 will most likely send you from an Indre By hotel for injuries or sudden illness
places.goodToKnow: Open 24h (+45 38 63 61 79) but referral via 1813 is expected. Bring passports and any EHIC/insurance papers · Location needs verification
places.address: Akutmodtagelsen, Ebba Lunds Vej 41E, 2400 København NV

places.name: Rigshospitalet (trauma centre + children's hospital)
places.near: Taxi ~10 min from Nørreport, or Metro M3 to Trianglen then ~10 min walk; ambulance/112 for life-threatening cases
places.notes: rigshospitalet.dk + Wikipedia (trauma centre role; ordinary ER handled by other hospitals), checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Denmark's most specialised hospital — the region's trauma centre, and home to the main paediatric department (BørneUngeKlinikken); 1813 typically directs sick children here from central Copenhagen
places.goodToKnow: Main phone +45 35 45 35 45. Not a walk-in ER for routine injuries (that's Bispebjerg) — children's acute cases go via 1813, severe trauma via 112

places.name: Møntvask Øster Farimagsgade (self-service laundry)
places.near: ~8 min walk northeast from Nørreport via Øster Farimagsgade
places.notes: Wanderlog place page (address, coords, reviews) + Yelp Møntvask listings, checked 2026-07-12
places.famousFor: Clean, English-signposted coin/card laundromat a short walk from Nørreport — 7 kg and 13 kg machines, big dryers, detergent included; ideal mid-trip wash for a family of 7
places.goodToKnow: Pay at a central station by machine number (card or coins); a 13 kg machine ≈75 DKK per wash, dryer ~12 DKK per 8 min; opens early morning. Budget ~1.5-2 hours

places.name: Elgiganten Fisketorvet (electronics)
places.near: S-train from Nørreport to Dybbølsbro (~7 min) then 5 min walk; mall also reachable by bus from Central Station
places.famousFor: Big central branch of Denmark's main electronics chain — chargers, adapters (Denmark uses type C/K plugs), power banks, headphones, camera cards; inside the Fisketorvet mall
places.goodToKnow: Phone +45 70 80 70 70. Mall has 100+ shops and food court — useful rainy-day option; Power (rival chain) is in Field's mall if you're near Ørestad · Location needs verification
